key_insight: Ethanol-assisted interfacial modification enables laser transmission welding of CFRP to fused silica without optical contact or mechanical clamping.
engineering_relevance: This offers a simpler, lower-fixturing route for joining carbon-fiber composites to transparent silica parts in precision manufacturing.
method: Treat the CFRP/silica interface with ethanol to alter interfacial conditions.
Use laser transmission welding to join the materials without direct optical contact.
Avoid clamp-based pressure during the welding process.
evidence_signal: The abstract only indicates the work is a journal article in Precision Engineering, so no quantitative performance data are provided here.
watch_item: Practical adoption may depend on how consistently ethanol modification controls interfacial quality across part geometries and surface conditions.
paper_title: Non-optical-contact and clamp-free laser transmission welding of CFRP to fused silica via ethanol-assisted interfacial modification
